anyOf.ask_user: Enter the folder to store results. Recommended: './../Results/[DATE]/[EXPERIMENT_NAME]/[RUN_NAME]/' (REMEMBER TO INCLUDE THE '/' AT THE END)

DISTANCE_TRANSMISSON_POWER_FOLDER.description: ANDRES: new storage locations for the distance-transmission power graph

anyOf.ask_user: Do you want to print verbose logs? (You might want to reduce simulation time) (yes/1/true)

PRINT_LOGS.description: Print verbose logs during the simulation.

PRINT_LOG_TOPOLOGY_METRICS.description: Log network topology details (NNC,AND,G,Nbs) into log_runX.out()

SNAPSHOT_CODE.description: Create a spanshot of the code in a RESULTS_FOLDER+"snapshot"

ENABLE_MCS_SNR.description: Use adaptive MCS based on SNR

CHANNEL_AWARE_ROUTE_COMPUTATION.description: Create link between a node pair based on current interference & GPS locations at both nodes. If false, only GPS locations are used to decide if a link exist between a node pair. Does not consider current interfence level. This is useful in presence of external interference.

ENABLE_PACKET_REINSERTION.description: If true, reinsert packets at source (as done in TCP)

anyOf.ask_user: Do you want a mobile scenario? Otherwise, a static scenario will be used? (yes/1/true)

MOBILE_SCENARIO.description: Disable to create static network topology

anyOf.ask_user: What is the path to the topology which you want to use?

STATIC_TOPOLOGY_PATH.description: Path to static topology file or folder. If using positions, path should be to file containing positions. If using time, path should be to a folder with Node{i}.txt, with "pos_x, pos_y" in each line for position at time corresponding to line number.

anyOf.ask_user: What routes do you want to force? Enter a list of pairs, e.g. [[1,4],[6,7]]

FORCED_ROUTES.description: Uses specified Routes instead of Target to Base Stations

anyOf.ask_user: What MCS do you want to force? {RouteNumber:{NodeID:MCS}}

EXT_INTERFERENCE.description: Node id -> External Interferece experienced by that node

ENABLE_Q_MNGMNT.description: If true, it will discard packets based on their survivability score, and rearrange them based on their survivability score and priority.

USE_FLOW_PRIORITY.description: If false, each flow has the same priroity. Else, flow priority = route index number, assigned at the start of the simulation.

STRICT_ROUTE_SELECTION.description: If true, routes with same cost are sorted by length, and IDs before being selected. Else, routes with same cost are selected randomly.

QUEUE_MANAGEMENT_PLUS.description: Enable code from Marcus regarding improvements on queue management

CSMA_CA_BACKOFF.description: Enable code from Marcus regarding reduced backoff in certain conditions

PRIORITY_BASED_ROUTING.description: Enable code from Marcus regarding route reservation and reshuffling route update based on priority

ENABLE_POWER_CONTROL.description: Ability to enable the power control scheme. Each node transmits the packets at the power necessary for the maximum distance link on the route (either next hop or previous hop)

ENABLE_CONGESTION_DETECTION.description: ANDRES: Ability to enable the modification of MCS and power depending on the congestion (NOT FINISHED YET)

CONGESTION_BASED_MCS.description: AT: Automatically detect congestion and increase or decrease MCS as necessary

ROUTES_WITH_CONGESTION_BASED_MCS.description: Index of routes of whose nodes will do congestion detection

CONGESTION_BASED_MCS_INCREMENT_THRESHOLD.description: Threshold to increment MCS idx when using congestion based MCS

CONGESTION_BASED_MCS_DECREMENT_THRESHOLD.description: Threshold to decrement MCS idx when using congestion based MCS

CONGESTION_BASED_MCS_INCREMENT_STEP.description: Steps to increment MCS idx by when incrementing using congestion based MCS

CONGESTION_BASED_MCS_DECREMENT_STEP.description: Steps to decrement MCS idx by when decrementing using congestion based MCS

ENABLE_INDIVIDUAL_PKT_SINR.description: Calculate SINR value for each individual packet in a combined packet, instead of the whole packet

MAX_MAC_PAYLOAD_LENGTH.description:
Sh: Max payload (MSDU) is 2304 bytes. +8 bytes for encryption using WEP. In MSDU, actual data from Application is 2048 bytes, 256 bytes for upper layer headers.
 Check two links: 1. Chris_Hyde's reply on Aug 5, 2005: https://www.cwnp.com/forums/posts?postNum=307311, 2. Table 3-1 at https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/80211ac-a-survival/9781449357702/ch03.html

BASE_PAYLOAD_LENGTH.description: Base payload length for a single packet. Used for computing the NAV duration.

USE_PACKET_AGGREGATION.description: Aggregate multiple packets into one based on MCS Index

PACKET_LOSS_RATE.description: Use 0.01 if 1% of packets are corrupted

anyOf.ask_user: What data rate do you want to use? Enter an array with a number (in Mbps) for each flow.

TARGET_PKT_GENERATION_RATE.description: Sh: 61 pkts/s @ 2 kB pkt size = 1 Mbps data rate (for payload)

PACKET_TTL.description: Sh: in ns. Use simpy.core.Infinity for non time sensitive flows.

MOBILITY_MODEL.description: "TCPIPE_BSCAP_" for TC-PIPE model, "BSCAP_" for BSCAP model.

anyOf.ask_user:
What is the size of the area you want to use? The area will be a square NxN meters.
BSCAP will attempt to spread the nodes throughout this area; choose number of nodes and area accordingly

AREA_X.description: in meters. Depends on the coordinates fed from files stored in node trajectories folder

AREA_Y.description: in meters. Depends on the coordinates fed from files stored in node trajectories folder

anyOf.ask_user: How many base stations should be on the map?

NUMBER_OF_BS.description: Number of Base Stations; Set routes using FORCED_ROUTES if 0 BS.

anyOf.ask_user: How many nodes (regular + BS) should be on the map?

MAX_QUEUE_SIZE.description: Max packets a node can store in its MAC queue. Use any value <= 0 for infinite buffer queue.

anyOf.ask_user: Enter the speed of nodes in m/s from: (20, 40, 50)

LLT_SAMPLE_FILE.description: .dat file with LLT samples for GM mobility model at a given transmission range (km) and node speed (m/s)

anyOf.ask_user: When should packet generation start? In seconds.

PKT_GENERATION_START_SEC.description: In seconds. Wait until setup is complete.

anyOf.ask_user: When should packet generation end? In seconds.

PKT_GENERATION_END_SEC.description: In seconds. Generate packets until this time.

PKT_GENERATION_START_TIME.description: in ns. Wait until setup is complete.

PKT_GENERATION_END_TIME.description: in ns. Generate packets until this time.

SIM_TIME.description: Sh: Simulation run time, in ns. Run for a little longer time (here, TTL + 1 s) to allow queued packets to reach destination node before terminating simulation.

MCS_SNR_TABLE.description: Example MCS SINR Table used by us

anyOf.ask_user: What MCS should data packets use by default?

STEP_SIZE.description: To reduce computation time. Higher value reduces resolution which can result in losing track of packets

RADIO_SENSITIVITY.description: power under which signal is not sensed. = -102 dBm # Sh: rx sensitivity must be lower than noise floor.

NOISE_FLOOR.description: 20 MHz BW @ 300 K Temp = -101 dBm. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Johnson%E2%80%93Nyquist_noise#:~:text=.-,Noise%20power%20in%20decibels,-%5Bedit%5D

MIN_MIN_REQUIRED_SINR.description: Sh: Min of min required SINR in the MCS-SNR table (at MCS0)

MAX_MIN_REQUIRED_SINR.description: Sh: Max of min required SINR in the MCS-SNR table (at MCS7)

ACK_LENGTH.description: Sh: Same as MAC header length except for the added fields in MAC header. AT: Added 1 byte for to acknowledge each packet in a combined packet

RTS_THRESHOLD.description: Sh: Use RTS-CTS if data payload size is bigger than the threshold

TCP_ENABLED.description: Switch between UDP (when false) and TCP (when true)

DELTA.description: In second. To prevent packet getting stuck at intermediate nodes due to link break

W1.description: Weights: normalized hop count and interfering links

W2.description: Weights: normalized hop count and interfering links

HC_THRESHOLD.description: Used to limit the maximum route length to find in the network. Increasing this value will increase computation time

SUR_THRESHOLD.description: Packets with survivability score less than this are discarded from the queue

FIRST_N_PKTS.description: Consider these many packets starting from head of the line to discard based on their survivability score

TC_PIPE_mobility_ON.description:
1. BSCAP model set TC_PIPE_mobility_ON = False 
2. TC-PIPE model set TC_PIPE_mobility_ON = True

NODE_FAILURE_PERCENTAGE.description: percent of nodes that fail in between 1000 and 3000s

MAX_INITIAL_ENERGY.description: Maximum initial energy for a node

NUM_FAILURES.description: Percentage of UAVs that fail between 1000s and 3000s

anyOf.ask_user: Enter the routing cost type: 1: Cost Formula, 2: Least Hops, 3: Closest BS from Target by distance

ROUTING_COST_TYPE.description: 1: Cost Formula based, 2: Hop count based, 3: Closest BS from Target by euclidean distance

anyOf.ask_user: Enter the target locations as an array of target locations of form [[x1, y1], [x2, y2], ...]:

TARGET_LOCATION.description: Array of target locations of form [[x1, y1], [x2, y2], ...]

anyOf.ask_user: Enter the position(s) of the base station(s) as an array of target locations of form [[x1, y1], [x2, y2], ...]:
